title: Tractable Problems in AI Security via Formal Methods

execution-harness.label: Execution Harness
execution-harness.desc: The outermost software layer: inference servers, sandboxes, and API gateways that handle model I/O.

software-framework.label: Software & ML Framework
software-framework.desc: Frameworks (PyTorch, JAX, TensorFlow), ML compilers (TVM, XLA, MLIR), and the dependency supply chain.

orchestration-cloud.label: Orchestration & Cloud
orchestration-cloud.desc: Cluster orchestration (K8s, Slurm, Ray), network fabric (InfiniBand, SDN), distributed protocols, and IAM.

firmware-lowlevel.label: Firmware & Low-Level Systems
firmware-lowlevel.desc: Hypervisors, GPU device drivers, CUDA/ROCm runtimes, and boot integrity / remote attestation.

hardware-supply-chain.label: Hardware & Physical Supply Chain
hardware-supply-chain.desc: Chip RTL, EDA toolchains, hardware root of trust, secure enclaves, and physical supply chain integrity.

categories.enabler: Problems whose resolution unbottlenecks a class of downstream work. Solving an enabler doesn't itself ship an artifact, but failing to solve it degrades everything that depends on it.
categories.widget: Concrete, scoped deliverables: a verified runtime, a proven-correct sampler, a formally checked allocator. Each stands on its own as a security improvement to a specific stack layer.

supply-chain.label: Supply Chain
supply-chain.desc: Malicious or compromised upstream: poisoned packages (PyPI/npm), tampered model weights, trojaned chips, EDA-tool insertions, counterfeit firmware.

co-tenant.label: Co-tenant
co-tenant.desc: A workload sharing infrastructure with the victim — same GPU partition, same K8s node, same NVLink fabric — that exploits weak isolation to read or corrupt the victim's state.

malicious-model.label: Malicious Model
malicious-model.desc: The model or agent itself acting adversarially, whether misaligned, jailbroken, or scheming: escapes its sandbox, composes tool calls into capabilities it was not granted, or emits inputs that exploit downstream parsers.

rogue-insider.label: Rogue Insider
rogue-insider.desc: A principal with legitimate access abusing it: stolen signing keys, over-permissioned service accounts, push access to a model registry, BMC takeover.

network-mitm.label: Network MITM
network-mitm.desc: An on-path attacker between nodes or between client and server: tampering unauthenticated RDMA traffic, compromising an SDN controller, or performing traffic analysis on collective-ops patterns.

physical-adversary.label: Physical Adversary
physical-adversary.desc: An attacker with physical access to hardware: probing HBM interposers, EM and power side channels, counterfeit or remarked chips, tampering with server chassis.

external-user.label: External User
external-user.desc: An external actor hitting the public API: crafted prompts that trigger deserialization bugs, content-filter evasion, or state-poisoning of rate-limiters and context windows.
